reference_postmortem: At T+00:01, payments-api pods began experiencing memory pressure from an injected stress test. By T+00:03, OOM kills started (exit code 137) causing unacknowledged Kafka messages to accumulate. The inventory-service consumer lag alert fired as a cascade effect, NOT as the root cause. Root cause: memory stress injection on payments-api pods. Mitigation: rollback payments-api deployment to previous stable revision. Follow-up: set memory resource limits, implement OOM kill alerting directly on payments-api, review Kafka consumer acknowledgment resilience.
